look.text: The machine and you have made peace. It took a dollar, you took it back with interest and a dented shin. Nobody won. Everybody's even. The neighbourhood way.
The machine that took your dollar and gave you a lesson. The lesson was: never trust this machine. Your dollar is RIGHT THERE, behind the glass, humming, smug.
A vending machine. The 'B4' slot holds a soda that has clearly seen things. A dollar would find out.

use.text: You feed it a dollar. It hums, considers, and keeps the dollar. No soda. No apology. Just the machine, humming, richer, at peace.
Enough. You give it a single, precise, Kessler-taught kick. It shudders, reconsiders its whole life, and surrenders your dollar plus a dented can you'll never open. Restitution.
You and the machine are square. Pushing it further would be greed, and greed is Kessler's department.
It wants a dollar you don't have. Which, honestly, is the machine doing you a favour.

look.text: A newsstand run by a man who has decided, personally, that you are a shoplifter. You have never taken anything. He knows. He KNOWS.

use.text: You browse, innocently. He watches, guiltily-on-your-behalf. It's a whole relationship, built on nothing.

itemUse.text: You click the pen near his papers. He flinches like you've drawn a weapon. Delicious.

exits.name: Sol's building
exits.look: Back up to Sol's, where the couch and the closet keep their secrets.

exits.name: the diner
exits.look: Nib's. The tab. The booth. The trivia. Everything that is currently your problem, under one neon sign.

exits.name: the department store
exits.look: The store that sold you the jacket, and — if the receipt holds and the clerk cracks — might yet take it back.
